summary: The user, a technical leader, scrutinized the Ficduct V2 roadmap, concluding that the January 2027 General Availability (GA) target is at high risk of delay. The user systematically cross-referenced technical and operational documents to build a case against the timeline's feasibility. Key risks identified include significant technical debt (a monolithic database), an aggressive 58% engineering hiring plan for specialized roles (Rust, ML), and already-missed sprint velocity targets. The session reinforced the user's prior conclusion that a disconnect exists between strong internal engineering and poor customer-facing tools, labeling the platform a 'black box' for users. A major point of contention was the discovery of marketing materials and a customer testimonial praising an 'AI-Assisted Mapping' feature that engineering documents confirm is a future V2 feature not yet in beta. The user asserted this discrepancy highlights a serious credibility issue and a fundamental disconnect between the ambitious on-paper plan and the reality of the engineering work required.
key_facts: The V2 General Availability (GA) target date is January 2027.
The V2 plan requires decomposing a monolithic PostgreSQL configuration database.
Current sprint velocity is 'At Risk', running at 208 story points against a 220 target.
The customer-facing 'Ficduct Insight' module has a low satisfaction score of 3.6/5 and requires a complete overhaul for V2.
The engineering team is planned to grow by 58% (from 41 to 65 engineers) by Q4 2026.
Hiring for senior Rust and ML engineers is a top-rated risk for the project.
The 'AI-Assisted Mapping' feature is marketed as a current capability with a customer testimonial, but is a new V2 feature planned for a Q3 2026 beta.
The V2 program is allocated 65% of engineering capacity from Q2 2026.
The user confirmed the internal observability stack (Prometheus, ELK) is mature, but its capabilities are not exposed to customers.
positions_taken: The V2 GA date of Q1 2027 is 'incredibly ambitious' and at 'high risk of delay'.
The gap between internal and external observability creates a 'black box' problem for customers.
The aggressive hiring plan for specialized roles is a 'major threat to the timeline'.
Marketing is 'selling the roadmap, not the product,' which creates a 'serious credibility issue'.
There is a significant disconnect between the confidence of the planning team and the reality of the engineering work required.
The 'Insight' module needs a 'complete overhaul', not just minor tweaks.
